name: Chocolate Chunk Brownies
recipeIngredient: 9 ounces swiss dark chocolate, cut into 1/4-inch chunks
recipeInstructions:
1. Position a rack in the center of the oven and preheat to 325 degrees F.
Line a 13-by-9 inch baking pan with double thickness of aluminum foil,
extending 2 inches beyond the two short ends. Fold the overhang down.
Butter the bottom of the foil-lined pan.
2. In a medium bowl, stir together flour, baking soda, baking powder, and salt.
3. Place semisweet chocolate in a large bowl.
4. In a medium saucepan, combine sugar, butter, corn syrup, and water.
Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon,
until the butter melts, sugar is dissolved, and mixture comes to boil.
Remove pan from heat and pour hot syrup over chocolate. Let stand
for 1-2 minutes to melt chocolate. Whisk until smooth.
5. One at a time, whisk in eggs, blending until smooth. Whisk in vanilla
and flour mixture, mixing until batter is smooth. Using rubber spatula,
fold in 1 cup walnuts and 6 ounces swiss chocolate chunks.
6. Scrape batter into prepared pan and spread evenly with spatula.
Sprinkle remaining 1/2 cup walnuts and 3 ounces chocolate over top.
7. Bake brownies for 40-50 minutes, or until a cake tester or toothpick
inserted in center comes out with few moist crumbs clinging to it.
8. Cool brownies in pan set on wire rack. When completely cool,
cover pan with plastic wrap and let set at room temperature
for at least 6 hours or overnight. Invert onto large plate.
Remove foil carefully, peel off, and invert again onto smooth cutting surface.
Cut into 24 bars. Store at room temperature in airtight container up to five days.
Allow brownies to set at room temperature for at least 6 hours before cutting.
